title: Change blueprint — implementation plan
question: Will this plan build the accepted specification safely?
lede: Gate B reviews how the accepted spec becomes code: the slices, the data flow, the riskiest surface, and the way out if it goes wrong. Approving it authorizes build-with-notes to start slice 1.
gateQuestion: Approve this plan as the build contract for the tag-filter change?
inputs: three-code-paths record — functional core, thin shell

specContext.acceptedVia: accepted in chat 2026-07-22 after Gate A review
specContext.summary: Single ?tag= filter over the static skills catalog; pure filterByTag testable in Node; chips as filter links; full catalog without JavaScript; no network, no multi-tag, no persistence.

slicesLede: Five vertical slices on the accepted functional-core shape; nothing is user-visible until slice 4, and every slice leaves node --test green.

slices.title: Pure filterByTag with its own assertions
slices.detail: filterByTag(skills, tag) lands as a plain importable function: null tag returns the full list, unknown tags return empty, matches filter by inclusion.

slices.title: Pure parseTagFromSearch
slices.detail: parseTagFromSearch(search) reads the single ?tag= parameter via URLSearchParams and returns the tag or null; no DOM involved.

slices.title: Static chip markup in the no-JS catalog
slices.detail: Every skill row renders its tags as plain ?tag= links so the page is complete and navigable before any script runs.

slices.title: Browser glue: read URL, filter, replaceChildren
slices.detail: app.mjs wires location.search through the two pure functions and re-renders the list; the empty state from interface-directions renders for unknown tags.

slices.title: Back/forward wiring and final sweep
slices.detail: popstate re-runs the same glue; acceptance criteria walked end to end at 1440 and 320, with and without JavaScript.

flow.lede: One direction of dependency: the DOM depends on pure functions, never the reverse. The dark node is the only stateful surface.
flow.caption: Solid = call path on every navigation. Dashed = the browser-only wiring added in slice 4; nothing to the left of it knows the DOM exists.

riskyCode.lede: The only tricky surface: the browser glue must fall back to the full catalog when the URL has no tag, without double-rendering on load.

risks.risk: Unknown ?tag= values render a blank list instead of the accepted empty state
risks.mitigation: Slice 4 renders emptyState(tag) whenever the filtered list is empty; acceptance criterion 3 checks it explicitly.

risks.risk: Chip links fight the JS re-render and cause a full page reload
risks.mitigation: Chips stay plain links; slice 5 intercepts clicks only when history.pushState is available, so no-JS behavior is untouched.
filterByTag preserves input order by construction; a test asserts stable ordering.

tests: filterByTag: null tag, matching tag, unknown tag, order preservation (4 assertions)
parseTagFromSearch: missing, present, duplicate parameters (3 assertions)
Static markup: every tag chip resolves to a real catalog tag (2 assertions)
Glue: unknown tag renders the empty state, popstate re-renders (3 assertions)
rollout: Slices land behind nothing — each is inert until slice 4 wires the glue
Slice 4 ships last, after 1–3 are green in CI
Manual sweep at 1440/320, JS on and off, before calling it done
rollback: Single revert of the slice-4 commit restores the static catalog exactly
Slices 1–3 are pure additions and can stay in place harmlessly

options.label: Approve the plan
options.consequence: build-with-notes is authorized to start slice 1 against this exact slice list.

options.label: Request changes
options.consequence: The plan reopens with your notes; no slice starts until Gate B is re-reviewed.

options.label: Reject the plan
options.consequence: The accepted spec stands, but this build approach is discarded; a new plan must be drafted.

recommendation.reason: Every slice is independently verifiable, the dependency direction matches the accepted three-code-paths decision, and rollback is a single revert.

unresolved: whether slice 5 intercepts chip clicks or lets them navigate (decided during build)
openStatusLabel: Gate B open — no resolution proposed yet
